title: Adoption Information Act
plain_summary: This bill says that family planning programs that get money from the government must give out special pamphlets about adoption. Anyone who asks about medical services or abortion must be given these pamphlets. The pamphlets will have contact information for adoption centers in their area. The government will make these pamphlets and give them to the family planning programs to hand out. The programs must pay for giving out these pamphlets using money they already have.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Energy and Commerce.

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Adoption Information Act.

sections.title: Section 2 Provision in Family Planning Services of Pamphlets Containing Adoption Center Contact Information
sections.text: This section adds new rules to Title Five of the Social Security Act. The new section five hundred fourteen says that the government can only give money or contracts to family planning programs if those programs make certain promises. The programs must promise to give every person who asks about medical services or abortion services a pamphlet when they first ask for help. This pamphlet must list all the adoption centers in their area. It must include the address and phone number for each adoption center. The programs must also give each person a chance to read this pamphlet. The Secretary of Health and Human Services must create these pamphlets, update them every year, and send them to all family planning programs. No extra money will be given to pay for this new requirement. Programs must use money they already receive to follow these rules.
